description: Holding a std::sync::Mutex guard across an .await point in async code, risking deadlock and breaking Send.
body: Failure mode: code locks a std Mutex and then awaits while holding the guard, so the future is no longer Send (won't spawn on a multi-thread runtime) or the same task re-enters and deadlocks. Detection signals: compiler error 'future cannot be sent between threads safely' mentioning MutexGuard; tasks hang under tokio's multi-thread scheduler; clippy await_holding_lock fires. Do not hold a std::sync lock across .await. Safer fallback: drop the guard before awaiting (scope it in a block), or use an async-aware tokio::sync::Mutex when the lock must span an await, then confirm the future is Send and the hang is gone.
